note: Exhaustive viewer-facing feature inventory (parity contract). 7 slices + adversarial completeness critic. Drives the FROZEN acceptance suite in acceptance/.

features.name: Tab-invariant page chrome (header above tab bar)
features.behavior: Every tab, including Explorer, sits under fixed chrome: an uppercase eyebrow line, a serif h1 with the word 'Winners' colored #E0B458, a subtitle paragraph (max-width 780), a 'New here? Tap any' row with an italic 'i' Expl button that toggles a 'How to read this dashboard.' explainer box, and a horizontal tab bar of 5 buttons (6 with BubbleGauge enabled: BG.tab {id:'bubblegauge', label:'AI Regime'} appended last). Active tab has a 2px solid #E0B458 bottom border, color #EDE8DC, fontWeight 700; inactive tabs #78829a. Default tab is 'explorer'. Page background #0E1526, content max-width 1060px. Footer 'Key sources:' block after tab content.
interactions: Click a tab button to switch tab content (Explorer unmounts — all Explorer local state including selected crisis and hidden-line toggles resets on return)
Click the 'i' button to expand/collapse the how-to explainer
exact_strings: An interactive atlas · common t−60 → t+60 month scale
Crisis Winners — assets that rose when markets collapsed
Stylized monthly reconstructions anchored to magnitudes documented in the peer-reviewed literature
not tick data, and not investment advice. Pre-1970 paths are schematic.

features.name: Crisis selector chip row (11 crises, default = GFC)
features.behavior: Horizontally scrollable row of 11 pill Chips, one per CRISES entry in array order, each rendering '{name} · {years}' (years in a 0.65-opacity span). Active chip border uses the crisis category color (CAT[c.cat].color), background rgba(224,180,88,0.12), text #EDE8DC bold; inactive chips have border rgba(237,232,220,0.16) and text #9AA3B5. DEFAULT SELECTED CRISIS IS 'gfc' (Global Financial Crisis) — NOT the first chip (ai2026). Chip order: AI Investment Bubble, Great Depression, Stagflation / Nifty Fifty, Japan Asset Bubble, Black Monday, Asian Financial Crisis, Dot-com Bust, Global Financial Crisis, European Sovereign Debt Crisis, COVID-19 Crash, South Sea Bubble.
interactions: Click a chip → setCid: header panel, banner presence, chart data/series, legend chips and sources line all swap to that crisis; base/log toggles and hidden map persist (hidden is keyed per crisis.id+key)
exact_values: CAT colors: A #D4A017, B #C0564A, C #8A6FB3, D #4A90A4, E #6B7F6B
features.acceptance_probe: on load expect 11 chip buttons in the stated order; expect h2 to read 'Global Financial Crisis' (default); click 'South Sea Bubble' chip → h2 becomes 'South Sea Bubble'

All cause/highlight/sources strings are frozen content per crisis (e.g. GFC highlight begins 'The full safe-haven set fired at once: gold strong safe haven at the peak (Baur & McDermott)…').

pure financial bubbles count fully (1.0), outside shocks like COVID count half
which asset the retrospectively smartest investor held

features.name: BubbleGauge Explorer seam: AiLivePanel mount + live re-anchoring + honest proxy relabeling
features.behavior: Three seams, all inert without the ?status-api gate: (1) MOUNT — {crisis.potential && BG.enabled && BG.AiLivePanel && <BG.AiLivePanel/>} renders the LIVE BACKFILL card directly under the POTENTIAL banner, only on the ai2026 crisis and only when the gate is on; the panel itself returns null when the feed is down (error-bounded, fallback null). (2) DATA — aiLive = BG.useAiLive() (null when gate off/feed down); in the chart data memo, for crisis.id==='ai2026' each series key present in aiLive.a replaces the static anchor array s.a with feed-derived anchors (feed points indexed i-60, FX pairs usdchf/usdjpy inverted to show the currency vs USD), then goes through the same interp/rebase pipeline. (3) LABELS — dispSeries relabels any ai2026 series with aiLive.live[key]===true to aiLive.labels[key] (AI_MAP labels, e.g. 'NASDAQ-100 (QQQ TR proxy) — feared market', 'Gold (GLD proxy) — lead regime-matched hedge', 'Swiss franc vs USD (inverted USD/CHF)', 'US dollar (Fed broad index — not DXY)'); relabel shows in both legend chips and tooltip line names. With gate off, ai2026 renders the static Jul-2026 snapshot with original labels and no panel.
interactions: Load with ?status-api=demo (offline fixture) → panel appears, legend chip labels change to proxy labels
Load without query param → no panel, static labels, zero network calls
exact_strings: US dollar (Fed broad index — not DXY)
exact_values: AI_MAP feed keys: qqq, gold, tbill3m_tr, ust10y_tr, usdchf(inv), usd_broad_index, usdjpy(inv), btc

features.name: Rebase segmented control (peak=100 vs t−60=100)
features.behavior: Two-option Seg control, default 'peak'. Options render 'Index: peak = 100' and 'Index: t−60 = 100'. Selected option has background #E0B458 with dark text #0E1526. 'peak' rebases every series so its interpolated value at month 0 equals 100; 'start' rebases so month −60 equals 100. Rebase is multiplicative (v * 100/vals[baseMonth+60]) applied per series after linear interpolation; rendered row values are +v.toFixed(1).
interactions: Click 'Index: t−60 = 100' → all lines re-scale to start at 100 at the left edge; tooltip numbers change accordingly (e.g. GFC S&P 500 at Peak reads 100 in peak mode, 190 in t−60 mode)
exact_values: GFC mkt at m=0: 100.0 (peak mode) / 190.0 (start mode)

features.name: Main line chart: axes, reference lines, per-series line styling incl. dashed safe havens and t0 truncation
features.behavior: Recharts LineChart in ResponsiveContainer height 370. Data = 121 monthly rows m=−60..+60; each series linearly interpolated between its anchor pairs [month,value], held flat before first/after last anchor, rebased, rounded toFixed(1). Series with an 'end' field (ALL 8 ai2026 series have end:0) render null for m > end, so every AI-2026 line stops exactly at t0 and the right half is empty. XAxis: numeric, domain [−60,60], ticks [−60,−48,−36,−24,−12,0,12,24,36,48,60], formatter renders 0 as 'Peak', positives as '+12' etc., negatives as '-12' (ASCII hyphen). Vertical dashed ReferenceLine at x=0 with label 'PEAK' (insideTopRight, 9px); horizontal dashed ReferenceLine at y=100. Lines: type monotone, dot=false, no animation; strokeWidth = s.w || (cls==='market' ? 2.6 : 1.9) — ai2026 gold has w:2.5; strokeDasharray '6 4' when s.dashed (all 7 ai2026 non-market lines plus COVID Bitcoin are dashed; all other historical series solid). Market line always #E05252. CartesianGrid horizontal only.
interactions: Chart re-renders on crisis/base/log/legend-toggle changes

features.name: Custom chart tooltip (sorted, rounded, fmtM month label)
features.behavior: Hover tooltip: dark box (#0B111F, max-width 250) with a header from fmtM(month): m=0 → 'Crisis peak · t0'; otherwise 't+{m} mo' or 't−{m} mo' (Unicode minus U+2212 for negatives, unlike the axis ticks' ASCII hyphen). Below, one row per non-null series sorted by value DESCENDING, line name left (ellipsized) and Math.round(value) right in the series color, tabular-nums. Null values (past a series' end) are filtered out. Returns nothing when no payload.

slices.notes: STATE SHAPE (Explorer, dashboard.jsx:509-539): cid ('gfc' default), base ('peak'|'start', default 'peak'), log (bool, default false), hidden ({[crisis.id + series.key]: true}) seeded from defaultOff flags (only covid+btc). All state is component-local — switching top-level tabs unmounts Explorer and RESETS everything (returns to GFC, peak, linear, Bitcoin re-hidden). DERIVED DATA PIPELINE (must be bit-identical for data assertions): interp() = per-month linear interpolation over anchor pairs [month,value] on m=−60..60, clamped flat outside the anchor range; rebase(vals, baseMonth) multiplies by 100/vals[baseMonth+60] (baseMonth 0 for 'peak', −60 for 'start'); rendered values +v.toFixed(1); tooltip displays Math.round. Series masking: rows[i][key]=null when s.end !== undefined && m > s.end — only ai2026 series carry end:0, so exactly the AI crisis truncates at t0. CRISES entry shape: {id, name, years, cat (A-E), weight, potential?, stylizedAll?, peak, cause, highlight, sources, series:[{key, label, cls (market|universal|category|unique|falsified), color, dashed?, defaultOff?, end?, w?, a:[[month,value],...]}]}. CLS tag glyphs: market '▼', universal 'U', category 'C', unique '★', falsified '!'. GOTCHAS: (1) axis tick negatives use ASCII '-' but tooltip fmtM uses Unicode minus '−' (t−12 mo) — tests must match the right glyph; (2) years strings use en-dashes ('2007–09') and '·' separators; (3) hidden map is keyed per-crisis so toggles survive crisis switches but not tab switches; (4) tooltip rows sorted by value descending and null-filtered; (5) the BG seam reads window.BubbleGauge at module scope into const BG — bubblegauge.jsx MUST load before dashboard.jsx; with no ?status-api param BG={enabled:false} and Explorer renders the static atlas with zero BG behavior; (6) aiLive replacement is per-series-key: a partially-available feed re-anchors some lines while others keep static anchors + original labels (footnote lists the static keys); (7) FX feed series (usdchf, usdjpy) are inverted (1/value) before anchoring so charts show the currency vs USD; (8) Recharts pinned 2.12.7 UMD — Line isAnimationActive=false everywhere (stable screenshots); (9) all colors/styles are inline styles, no CSS classes except .tabbar-scroll. The frozen-content rule in CLAUDE.md means every string/number above is contract-frozen; the golden data hash guards CRISES.

slices.notes: Corrections to the slice brief: (1) The matrix is 13 asset rows x 11 crisis columns (MATRIX has 13 entries, dashboard.jsx:261-288), not 11x11. (2) There is NO hover tooltip on matrix cells — the interaction is click-to-select (sel={r,c} local useState) which gold-borders the cell and reveals a note panel below the table; blank (-1) cells are still clickable buttons (cursor 'default' but onClick attached) and show the fallback sentence; there is no deselect. (3) PAIRS/PairTile (dashboard.jsx:775-834) are defined adjacent to the Matrix code but render exclusively on the AGGREGATE tab (line 906, inside mode==='pairs'); a rewrite must place them there. (4) PairTile does NOT show correlations or the matrix data — it shows rebased index line charts from buildAggregate() composites plus AI-2026 overlays (ai_mkt/ai_cash/ai_ust/ai_au columns, null for m>0). Pair 4 (Gold) uses histKey 'gold' which buildAggregate accumulates separately (series key 'au'/'aul' double-counted into both 'category' and 'gold' families). State shape: Matrix has one state atom sel:{r,c}|null; each Expl has independent boolean open state; all state is component-local and resets on tab switch. Derived data gotcha: PairTile stats use data[0] (m=−60) and data[96] (m=+36) by hard index; the rendered '+{aiRun}%' template produces the literal string '2026 +-5%' for the Duration bonds tile (negative value after hardcoded '+') — a faithful rewrite must reproduce this quirk. Verified stats computed by executing lines 4-431 of dashboard.jsx in Node: market +61/+96/56, cash +19/+19/116, bonds +23/−5/121, gold +27/+130/138. bubblegauge seam: Aggregate calls BG.useAiLive() when enabled, which can re-anchor the AI overlay anchors (aiLive.a[key]) — with no ?status-api param BG={enabled:false} and the static anchors from CRISES are used; acceptance tests for parity should run without the query param. Typography is load-bearing throughout: en-dashes (–), minus signs (−), right single quotes (’), middle dots (·), ✓/✗/~/?/★/━/╌ glyphs appear in rendered strings and must survive the rewrite byte-for-byte. All exact colors: panel bg #141D31, page bg #0E1526, accent gold #E0B458, verification teal #5AA9A3.

features.name: buildAggregate composite data (7 families)
features.behavior: Derived dataset of 121 rows m=−60..+60. Families: market, cash, bonds, convex, category, unique, gold. Per crisis (excluding the potential ai2026 crisis and falsified series): series are interp'd to monthly, rebased to 100 at m=0, averaged within family, then crisis-weight-averaged across crises; values rounded toFixed(1). Gold = keys 'au'/'aul' and those series are double-counted: they feed both the 'category' family (via cls) and the 'gold' family. subFamily maps: cls market→market, unique→unique, universal key 'cash'→cash, universal keys cta/vol/puts→convex, other universal→bonds, remaining (category)→category. All families equal exactly 100 at m=0.

row m=+36: market 55.8, cash 116.2, bonds 121.4, convex 150.3, category 129.6, unique 389.6, gold 137.7
row m=+60: market 65.2, cash 120.4, bonds 136.2, convex 151.8, category 152.7, unique 424.9, gold 169.2

features.name: runFan determinism contract
features.behavior: Fan simulation must be bit-reproducible: mulberry32 PRNG seeded 7/11/13, 1500 sims, 6-month block bootstrap of the t0..t+60 log-returns of the 3 template series (returns taken from indices 60..119 of the interp+rebase(0) log path), start value 100, H=60. Quantile = sort ascending, take a[min(len−1, floor(f*len))] (a floor-index quantile, NOT interpolated). Rows: {m, b80:[q.10,q.90], b50:[q.25,q.75], med:q.50}; stats: mdd/mddLo/mddHi = q(.5/.25/.75) of per-path max drawdown (min/100−1), med36/lo36/hi36 = q(.5/.10/.90) of value at month 36, below36 = share of paths <100 at month 36. Any change to PRNG, quantile method, block size, or return extraction changes every displayed fan number.
features.acceptance_probe: Data-level test: re-implement or import runFan, run with seed 7 on [dotcom/mkt, depression/mkt, japan/mkt] and assert stats round-trip to the exact displayed strings in agg-16 (e.g. Math.round(med36)===62, (below36*100).toFixed(0)==='79').

slices.notes: STATE SHAPE: Aggregate holds mode ('pairs' default | 'all') and hidden ({lineKey:bool}, shared across both AGG_LINES and OVERLAY_LINES chips; persists across mode switches within the tab, resets on tab unmount). Fans live on the Analytics tab (useMemo, deps []) even though this slice covers them. DERIVED DATA: composite rows come from buildAggregate() — 121 rows m=−60..60, toFixed(1) — plus ai_* overlay keys injected in a useMemo keyed on aiLive; overlays are null for m>0 by design (dashed lines end at t0). GOTCHAS a rewrite must reproduce: (1) the PairTile bonds stat literally renders '2026 +-5%' because the template hardcodes '+' before a signed Math.round value; (2) gold series (keys au/aul) are double-counted into both 'category' and 'gold' composites; potential crisis (ai2026) and falsified series (btc/jpy) are excluded from all historical composites; (3) PairTile run() uses truthiness (first[k] ? …) not null-check; data[96] is m=+36; (4) character set matters: U+2212 '−' in 't−60', stat cards ('−55.9%'), and axis labels vs ASCII '-' from toFixed in fan stats ('-63%'); '…' in IQR; en-dash in '10–90%' and FanTip ranges; '↔' and '’' (curly apostrophe in 'AI ’26'); (5) fan determinism: mulberry32 + floor-index quantile + 6-month block bootstrap, seeds 7/11/13, sims 1500 — verified by Node execution of the pure-JS slice (lines 4-431 of /home/user/save-haven-monitor/dashboard.jsx): market '-63% (IQR -77%…-44%) · median 62, P 79%, [27,133]'; gold '-6% (IQR -17%…0%) · median 132, P 20%, [88,210]'; bonds '0% (IQR 0%…0%) · median 123, P 0%, [113,131]'; (6) composite spot values for data assertions: m=−60 {market 62.2, cash 84.3, bonds 81.4, convex 107.5, category 79.6, unique 102.1, gold 78.7, ai_mkt 51, ai_au 43.5, ai_cash 83.7, ai_ust 105.3}; m=+36 {market 55.8, cash 116.2, bonds 121.4, convex 150.3, category 129.6, unique 389.6, gold 137.7}; m=+60 {market 65.2, cash 120.4, bonds 136.2, convex 151.8, category 152.7, unique 424.9, gold 169.2}; (7) bubblegauge seam: BG = window.BubbleGauge || {enabled:false}; Aggregate calls BG.useAiLive() only when enabled — with a live feed the ai_* overlays and hence PairTile '2026 +N%' run-up numbers CHANGE (they are recomputed from re-anchored series), so exact-value assertions on ai_* must run without ?status-api or with the offline 'demo'/fixture key semantics accounted for; LiveBadge renders nothing while loading, and no badge exists at all without the gate; (8) charts are Recharts with isAnimationActive={false} everywhere (screenshot-stable) and ResponsiveContainer fixed heights 185/380/230; ChartTip sorts rows descending by value and drops nulls.

slices.notes: STATE SHAPE (Playbook component, dashboard.jsx:1499-1503): four independent useState values — mSel (selected method chip, string, DEFAULT 'M9'), vSel (verdict-matrix selected row index or null, DEFAULT null = no note shown), phase ('p0'|'p1'|'p2'|'p3', DEFAULT 'p0'), filter ('all'|'✓'|'±'|'✗', DEFAULT 'all'). ExpertRow keeps its own local open boolean (default false), so expansion is per-row and multiple rows can be open; filtering unmounts rows and loses their open state. None of this state persists (no URL/sessionStorage) and it resets when the user leaves and re-enters the tab (component remount). DERIVED DATA: cellSt(v) maps cell code→colors (P/P* green, F red, C/S amber, else transparent; fg always #0E1526); agCol map {✓:#7fbf94, ±:#d9b45c, ✗:#e08a8a} is passed into ExpertRow as a prop; bias color thresholds in ExpertRow are bias<=2 green, <=3 amber, else red; expert score bar width = score/66*100%. GOTCHAS FOR A REWRITE: (1) PB_COLS has 9 columns and omits M0 and merges 'M3/4' — verdict cells key off these exact column strings, and rows sparsely populate cells (missing → empty transparent chip, still rendered as a 26x22 bordered span). (2) 'P*' is styled identically to 'P' but the asterisk text must survive (Defense M7). (3) 'S' (Utilities M7 'split') styles like 'C'. (4) Phase 0/3 share one render path gated by (phase==='p0'||phase==='p3'); bar segment percent label suppressed when w<10 (only CHF 5% in p0). (5) Elink returns null for unknown keys and CWB (rank 24) has et:[] — the pill wrapper div is conditionally omitted only when et is empty. (6) Egress parity: the entire tab's only external hrefs are https://www.etoro.com/markets/{slug}; 5 ET entries (wgld, gold, phys, gdx, acwi) are defined but never referenced by any et array, so their URLs never appear in the DOM — a rewrite that renders them would change egress surface. Irregular slugs to not 'fix': ilf.us, vt.us, copper.fut, brk.b, plus .de/.l suffixed UCITS lines. (7) Method chips render '{id} ⛔' with a space before the emoji for the 4 veto methods; the veto styling also affects unselected chip border/text color. (8) The filter button labels ('All 24', '✓ agree', '± partial', '✗ diverge') differ from the filter values ('all','✓','±','✗') — tests should target labels. (9) All copy uses typographic characters (—, ·, →, ≈, −, ¥, ↓, ↗, ⛔, ✓, ±, ✗) that must be preserved byte-for-byte; the frozen-content rule in CLAUDE.md forbids editing any of these constants. (10) Expl is a shared component (dashboard.jsx:467-487) with aria-label 'What is this?' — used 5 times in this tab; each instance is independent toggle state. Agreement-glyph row counts for filter assertions: ✓=10, ±=11, ✗=3, total 24.

features.name: CDN/script failure fallback (fail-visible)
features.behavior: An inline IIFE (runs before any CDN script) registers window.addEventListener('error', cwFail, true) [capture — so it also catches resource load errors on the CDN <script> tags] and window.addEventListener('unhandledrejection', cwFail). cwFail: if #cw-loading still exists, its textContent is replaced with the failure message; else if #root exists AND has no firstChild, it injects a centered div and sets its textContent to the message (textContent — no HTML injection). Once the app has rendered (loading div gone, root populated) cwFail is a no-op, so post-load runtime errors do not clobber the UI. Triggers: any CDN script 404/integrity failure, any uncaught JS error during load, any unhandled promise rejection.
interactions: Block unpkg.com → page shows the failure message instead of a blank screen
exact_strings: The dashboard failed to load — please refresh, or check the browser console for details.

Stylized monthly reconstructions anchored to magnitudes documented in the peer-reviewed literature (Baur & McDermott 2010; Baele et al. 2020; Ranaldo & Söderlind 2010; Temin & Voth 2004; Gorton & Rouwenhorst 2006; Moskowitz, Ooi & Pedersen 2012; Conlon & McGee 2020; Reinhart & Rogoff 2009; Jordà et al. 2019) — not tick data, and not investment advice. Pre-1970 paths are schematic. The 2026 AI-bubble panel is a POTENTIAL crisis with its peak anchored at today by construction — real 2021–26 market backfill, lines end at t0, no forward projection (BIS, ECB, Fed & BoE 2026 stability reports). The Analytics tab adds the algorithmic battery (clock matching, bootstrap outcome fans, tail tests, regime & explosiveness diagnostics); the Playbook tab maps the ex-ante identification protocol (M0–M10) onto today's expert buy-list.
features.acceptance_probe: expect h1 to have text 'Crisis Winners — assets that rose when markets collapsed' with span 'Winners' colored rgb(224,180,88); expect the subtitle paragraph text to equal the frozen string (whitespace-normalized).

features.name: 'New here?' intro row with Expl info button
features.behavior: A flex-wrap row: eyebrow text 'New here? Tap any' followed by an Expl 'i' button (17x17 round, gold border, italic Georgia 'i', aria-label 'What is this?'). Clicking toggles: button fills #E0B458 with navy 'i' when open, and a full-width (flexBasis 100%) explainer box appears below (bg rgba(224,180,88,0.07), gold border, 11.5px #D9DCE4 text) containing the 'How to read this dashboard.' explainer. Clicking again closes it.
interactions: Click the 'i' button → explainer box expands to full row width; click again → collapses
exact_strings: Every crisis is drawn on the same clock: month 0 is the moment the market hit its highest point ("the peak"). Everything left of the dashed line is the boom before; everything right is what happened after. All lines are re-scaled to start from the same value, so you compare shapes, not prices. Red always = the market that crashed; gold/green/blue = things that went UP or held value. Dashed lines = today's (2026) situation, which by design stops at the peak line — nobody knows the right half yet. Every section has one of these "i" buttons with a plain-language explanation.
features.acceptance_probe: click(getByRole('button',{name:'What is this?'}).first()); expect text 'How to read this dashboard.' visible; click again; expect it hidden.

features.name: Tab bar: order, labels, active styling, default tab
features.behavior: Five tab buttons in fixed order: Crisis Explorer, Similarity Matrix, Aggregate, Analytics, Playbook. When BG.enabled, a 6th 'AI Regime' tab (BG.tab) is APPENDED after Playbook. Default active tab is 'explorer' (Crisis Explorer). Active tab: borderBottom '2px solid #E0B458', color #EDE8DC, fontWeight 700; inactive: transparent 2px bottom border, color #78829a, weight 400. Bar is display:flex gap 6, borderBottom 1px rgba(237,232,220,0.1), overflowX auto with className 'tabbar-scroll' and WebkitOverflowScrolling touch (scrolls horizontally on narrow screens, no wrap — whiteSpace nowrap, fontSize 13.5).
interactions: Click a tab button → it becomes active (gold underline, bright text) and its panel replaces the previous one
Only one tab panel is rendered at a time (conditional render, not display toggling)

Assert 'Crisis Explorer' has border-bottom-color rgb(224,180,88) on load; click 'Analytics' → styles swap and Analytics content mounts.

When BG.enabled: <BG.Strip goToDetail={() => setTab('bubblegauge')} /> mounts BETWEEN the intro row and the tab bar (strip is above the tab bar); tab==='bubblegauge' renders <BG.DetailTab goToCrisis={() => setTab('explorer')} />. So a strip CTA can jump to the AI Regime tab, and the detail tab can jump back to Crisis Explorer. Tab state is not persisted (useState, resets to explorer on reload) and there is no URL routing.

Starts 'Key sources: Baur & McDermott (2010) J. Banking & Finance · Baele, Bekaert, Inghelbrecht & Wei (2020) RFS · ...' and ends '... Campbell & Shiller (1998) JPM · IEA (2025) · SIPRI (Apr 2026) · NATO (2025).' Full frozen list includes ~45 citations including 'BIS Annual Economic Report (Jun 2026) · ECB FSR (May 2026) & Int’l Role of the Euro (Jun 2026) · Fed FSR (May 2026) · BoE FSR (2026) · WGC (Jun 2026)'.

features.name: interp / rebase / fmtM (core series construction)
features.behavior: interp(anchors): produces exactly 121 values for months m=-60..+60 by piecewise-LINEAR interpolation between [month,value] anchor pairs, FLAT-CLAMPED to the first anchor value before anchors[0][0] and to the last after the final anchor. rebase(vals, baseMonth): multiplies all values by 100/vals[baseMonth+60] so the chosen month reads exactly 100. fmtM(m): m===0 → 'Crisis peak · t0'; positive → 't+N mo'; negative → 't−N mo' using the Unicode minus U+2212, e.g. fmtM(-12)==='t−12 mo', fmtM(12)==='t+12 mo'. These functions feed every chart, so any rewrite must reproduce them bit-for-bit.
features.acceptance_probe: page.evaluate golden: recompute interp on a frozen anchor set and assert the known interior value 124.9167 (4dp) and clamped ends; assert a chart X tooltip/label renders 'Crisis peak · t0' at m=0 and 't−12 mo' at m=-12.

features.name: Statistics helpers: zArr / corrArr / xcorrRow
features.behavior: logPath = elementwise Math.log. ser(id,key) resolves anchors from CRISES. zArr: z-scores with POPULATION std dev (divide by n), falling back to sd=1 when sd===0. corrArr: Pearson r as mean of products of z-scores (divide by n). xcorrRow(curLog, histLog, K): current window = zArr of curLog.slice(61-K, 61) (the K months ENDING at t0 inclusive); for each phase-shift p from -30 to +6 (37 candidates), window end e=p+60, start st=e-K+1, skipped if st<0 or e>120; emits {p, r} with r rounded to 3 decimals via toFixed(3) then unary +. These drive the Similarity Matrix and clock-matching numbers, so rounding and window boundaries must match exactly.
features.acceptance_probe: Data assertion (page.evaluate or extracted-module unit test): xcorrRow on frozen inputs reproduces frozen {p,r} rows to 3dp; corrArr(a,a)===1 within fp tolerance.

features.name: mulberry32 PRNG + runFan bootstrap simulator (deterministic)
features.behavior: mulberry32(seed) is the standard 32-bit mulberry32 generator — the exact bit-ops matter because runFan output is seed-deterministic and rendered as stats. runFan(specs, seed, sims=1500, block=6, H=60): for each [crisisId, seriesKey] spec builds monthly log-returns from rebase(interp(series),0) over months t0..t0+H (lp[i+1]-lp[i] for i=60..119); simulates 1500 paths starting at exactly 100 (log 100), block-bootstrap: pick a random return series, random start in [0, len-block], copy 6 consecutive returns, repeat to length 60; tracks min for max-drawdown and the value at month 36. Quantile q(arr,f) = sorted[min(n-1, floor(f*n))] (floor-index, NOT interpolated). Returns rows [{m, b80:[q.10,q.90], b50:[q.25,q.75], med:q.50}] for m=0..60 and stats {mdd(=q.5 of drawdowns), mddLo(.25), mddHi(.75), below36 (share of month-36 values <100), med36(.5), lo36(.10), hi36(.90)}. Deterministic goldens (as rendered): fan t0 median = 100 always; market fan med36 = 62, gold fan med36 = 132, bonds fan med36 = 123; market median max-drawdown = -0.6307 (rendered -63%-ish per formatting in Analytics slice).
exact_values: goldens: market med36=62, gold med36=132, bonds med36=123, market mdd=-0.6307, t0 median=100
features.acceptance_probe: page.evaluate recomputation with the app's own seeds: assert med36 values {market:62, gold:132, bonds:123} and market mdd -0.6307 as rendered in the Analytics fan stat labels; assert fan chart median at m=0 is 100.

features.name: subFamily classification + buildAggregate composite
features.behavior: subFamily(s): cls 'market'→'market'; 'falsified'→null (EXCLUDED from composites); 'unique'→'unique'; 'universal'→ key 'cash'→'cash', keys 'cta'/'vol'/'puts'→'convex', else 'bonds'; anything else→'category'. buildAggregate(): classes in fixed order ['market','cash','bonds','convex','category','unique','gold']; skips crises with potential:true (AI-2026 never enters historical composites); per crisis, each series is rebase(interp(s.a),0) (t0=100); series with key 'au' or 'aul' are ALSO added to the 'gold' class (double membership: gold counts in its subFamily AND in 'gold'); per-crisis equal-weight mean within class, then across crises weighted by cr.weight; output 121 rows {m:-60..60, <class>: value toFixed(1) as number, or null if class empty}. Golden values: aggregate market composite at m=-60 → 62.2 and at m=+48 → 62.4; every class at m=0 → 100.0 by construction.
exact_values: goldens: market m=-60 → 62.2, market m=+48 → 62.4, all classes m=0 → 100
features.acceptance_probe: Data assertion via page.evaluate or hovering the Aggregate chart at m=-60 / m=+48 and reading tooltip values 62.2 / 62.4 for the market composite; tooltip at m=0 reads 100 for every plotted class.

features.name: Category and class legend constants (CAT / CLS)
features.behavior: CAT maps crisis categories A-E to exact labels+colors: A 'Equity / tech bubble' #D4A017; B 'Real-estate / credit bubble' #C0564A; C 'Classic mania' #8A6FB3; D 'Currency / sovereign' #4A90A4; E 'Liquidity / exogenous shock' #6B7F6B. CLS maps series classes to tag glyph + name: market '▼' 'Collapsing market'; universal 'U' 'Universal winner'; category 'C' 'Category-specific winner'; unique '★' 'Crisis-unique winner'; falsified '!' 'Falsified safe haven'. These strings/glyphs appear in legends, chips, and matrix labels across tabs.
features.acceptance_probe: Assert legend/label elements render the five category labels with matching computed colors, and the class tag glyphs ▼/U/C/★/! appear alongside their names.

slices.notes: STATE SHAPE: the whole shell has ONE piece of state — const [tab,setTab]=useState('explorer'). No routing, no persistence of tab (bubblegauge's own key persistence via sessionStorage 'status-api' is a separate slice). tabs is derived per render: BG.enabled ? TABS.concat([BG.tab]) : TABS. LOAD-ORDER GOTCHA: dashboard.jsx captures BG = window.BubbleGauge || {enabled:false} at module-eval time (dashboard.jsx:7) — a rewrite must guarantee bubblegauge evaluates first (index.html script order) or the integration silently disables; also note the defensive `typeof window !== 'undefined'` guard. BRIDGE: dashboard reads exactly 7 properties {enabled, tab, Strip, DetailTab, useAiLive, AiLivePanel, LiveBadge}; bubblegauge also exports {demo, apiBase} which dashboard ignores. useAiLive is a React HOOK called conditionally as `(BG.enabled && BG.useAiLive) ? BG.useAiLive() : null` — legal only because BG is constant for the page lifetime; a rewrite must preserve that invariant or restructure. DERIVED DATA: everything chart-facing flows through interp (121-point linear, flat-clamped ends) → rebase (÷ value at base month ×100). Two rebase bases exist: t0 (index 60) for matrix/aggregate/analytics and a UI toggle peak|start in Explorer. buildAggregate excludes potential:true crises and falsified series, double-counts au/aul into a synthetic 'gold' class, weights by cr.weight, rounds toFixed(1). runFan is fully deterministic given (specs, seed): mulberry32 bit-exact PRNG, block=6 bootstrap, sims=1500, floor-index quantiles — goldens: t0 median 100, market med36=62 / gold 132 / bonds 123, market mdd -0.6307; agg market m=-60 62.2, m=+48 62.4; interp interior golden 124.9167. Number formatting gotchas: Unicode minus U+2212 in fmtM ('t−12 mo') and in header eyebrow ('t−60 → t+60'); toFixed(3) for correlations, toFixed(1) for aggregate. INDEX.HTML gotchas: error listener uses capture=true (catches CDN resource errors); cwFail sets textContent (no innerHTML injection of the message); noscript <style> hides #cw-loading; recharts pin uses umd/Recharts.js because 2.12.x ships no .min.js; loading text uses … entity; favicon is inline data-URI (no favicon.ico request). Site must work at any base path — all app URLs relative. Recharts named exports actually used by the shell slice imports (dashboard.jsx:2-3): LineChart, Line, XAxis, YAxis, CartesianGrid, Tooltip, ReferenceLine, ResponsiveContainer, Area, ComposedChart.

features.name: Activation resolution (?status-api param, key regex, sessionStorage, off-switch, base derivation)
features.behavior: IIFE runs before dashboard.jsx. PARAM='status-api', KEY_RE=/^[a-z0-9-]{1,32}$/ (no dots/URLs), SS_KEY='bubblegauge:enabled', DEMO_KEYS={demo:true,fixture:true}. Resolution order: (1) if '?status-api-off' present (any value, even empty) -> sessionStorage.removeItem(SS_KEY) and disable; (2) read ?status-api=<key>; if key matches KEY_RE persist to sessionStorage; (3) if param ABSENT, fall back to sessionStorage (tab persistence across navigations); an INVALID present param does NOT fall back to sessionStorage — it disables. (4) key in DEMO_KEYS -> {demo:true, base:null}. (5) hostname localhost/127.0.0.1/*.local -> base 'http://localhost:8000'. (6) else base='https://'+key+'.'+parent where parent = host with leftmost label stripped only when host has >2 labels, otherwise host itself (simple leftmost-strip, no public-suffix handling).
interactions: Visit ?status-api=demo then navigate without param: still enabled (sessionStorage)
Visit ?status-api-off: disabled and sessionStorage cleared
Visit ?status-api=Bad.Key!: disabled even if sessionStorage held a valid key
features.acceptance_probe: page.goto('/?status-api=demo'); expect strip visible; page.goto('/') (no param, same tab) strip still visible; page.goto('/?status-api-off') strip absent and sessionStorage['bubblegauge:enabled'] null; evaluate window.BubbleGauge.apiBase for non-demo keys.

Click strip -> switches to bubblegauge tab

features.name: Fetch layer: cache/TTL/timeout/503-warming/focus-revalidate/demo short-circuit
features.behavior: Module-level cache {path->{t,json}}, TTL 25*60*1000 ms; cached success returned without network. bgFetch: AbortController timeout 6000 ms; headers {accept:'application/json'}; status 503 -> {status:503,json:null} (warming, not error); other non-ok -> error 'HTTP <status>'; network/abort -> status 0 with error string. useEndpoint(path,fixture,validate) state {loading,notReady,error,json}: DEMO mode sets json=fixture synchronously in effect and NEVER fetches; live mode: 503->notReady, non-200/null/validate-fail->error 'bad payload' or fetch error, success->json. window 'focus' listener revalidates with {noCache:true} and only replaces state on validated 200 (failures silently keep current content). Endpoints: useScore '/api/v1/score' (validScore), useHistory '/api/v1/score/history?granularity=daily&limit=1000' (data is Array), useStatus '/api/v1/status' (has science_audit), useFeed '/api/v1/dashboard/feed' (validFeed).
interactions: window focus event triggers cache-bypassing refetch (live mode only)
features.acceptance_probe: Route-mock the 4 endpoints; assert 503 on /api/v1/score renders warming strip; assert malformed JSON renders unavailable strip; assert demo mode issues zero fetches; dispatch window focus event and count a second (no-cache) request.

features.name: Boundary validators (validScore/isNum/pair/validFeed/validFearGreed, FG_RATINGS/FG_ZONES)
features.behavior: isNum = finite number. pair = 2-element array of finite numbers. validScore requires: data+meta present; numeric headline_median; iqr & band_5_95 pairs; action_band string; block_S.indicators & block_D.indicators; red_flag_detail object + numeric red_flag_count; trend_states, fast_alarm, V present — else score payload rejected (strip/tab show unavailable). validFeed requires data.series object, data.metrics object, string data.anchor_month. validFearGreed: metric available && numeric value in [0,100] AND (detail.rating null OR one of FG_RATINGS ['extreme fear','fear','neutral','greed','extreme greed']) — else the whole Fear&Greed block is absent (dropped, not rendered). FG_ZONES=[25,45,55,75]. FG_COLORS: extreme fear #E05252, fear #C0564A, neutral #C7CBD6, greed #7fbf94, extreme greed #5AA9A3.
features.acceptance_probe: Mock feed with fear_greed value 105 or rating 'panic' -> assert no 'CNN Fear & Greed' text in LIVE BACKFILL card; mock /api/v1/score missing trend_states -> strip shows 'gauge unavailable'.

features.name: Strip: 4 states + loaded content (score, IQR, band pill, flags, trend, coverage, judgment, Open)
features.behavior: An <aside aria-label='AI bubble regime gauge'> above the tab bar. States: loading -> eyebrow 'AI regime gauge' + 'loading…'; 503 -> 'AI regime gauge' + 'warming up — no snapshot computed yet' (dimmed 0.85); error/invalid -> 'AI regime gauge' + 'gauge unavailable' (dimmed). Loaded (clickable, role=button, tabIndex 0, Enter/Space activates): rounded median in band color (demo '40'); 'IQR 34–47' (rounded, en-dash); band pill (demo 'HOLD'); micro copy 'Regime heuristic — not a probability, not advice.'; GaugeBar h=12; flags Pill '0/4 flags' (color red if count>=3, gold if >0, else muted; title = fired REDFLAG_COPY joined ' · ' else 'no override flags fired'); trend Pill 'Trend SPY IN · QQQ IN' (red if SPY faber OUT else green; title 'Faber 10-month trend rule (execution trigger)'); CoverageChip: 'Degraded' (#E8853D) if coverage.degraded else 'Coverage 100%' (#7fbf94, pct = min(S,D)*100 rounded; title=COPY.coverageTip); Freshness; judgment_call.text truncated at 220 chars + '…' with ' · stale' suffix when stale/error_class; trailing 'Open ›'. Click anywhere -> goToDetail() (switches to bubblegauge tab).
interactions: Click strip -> AI Regime tab opens
Keyboard Enter or Space on strip -> same
Hover flags/trend/coverage pills -> title tooltips
exact_strings: warming up — no snapshot computed yet
Regime heuristic — not a probability, not advice.

features.behavior: role='img' with aria-label 'Bubble regime <round(value)> of 100, IQR <lo> to <hi>, action band <band-lowercase>' (IQR clause omitted if not a pair). Background 3 zones: 0–45% blue rgba(91,141,239,0.16), 45–60% gold rgba(224,180,88,0.18), 60–100% red rgba(224,82,82,0.18). IQR band overlay in band color at 0.35 opacity from clamp(iqr[0])% to clamp(iqr[1])%; 3px median marker at clamp(value)%. Heights: strip 12, headline 16.

features.name: Band + grounding semantics maps
features.behavior: BAND: hold->{label 'HOLD', #5B8DEF}, trim->{'TRIM', #E0B458}, 'de-risk'->{'DE-RISK', #E05252}, 'suppressed (block degraded)'->{'SUPPRESSED', #9AA3B5}; unknown band falls back to hold. bandOneLiner copy per band (see exact_strings; suppressed: 'Not scored right now — too many inputs for one block are unavailable, so the action band is withheld rather than guessed.'). GROUND chips: literature-grounded #7fbf94 'Backed by peer-reviewed research.'; literature-adjacent #5AA9A3 'Motivated by the research, with a reasoned (not directly fitted) mapping.'; judgmental #d9b45c 'Reasoned expert choice, not a fitted model.'; contested #E8853D 'Known to misfire — deliberately down-weighted.'; lagging-confirmation #9AA3B5 'Confirms stress already underway; does not predict.'
interactions: Hover grounding pill -> tooltip text
exact_strings: Hold — structural risk is present but not acute. No action indicated by the trend rule.
Trim — fragility is elevated. Consider easing risk if the trend rule confirms.
De-risk — fragility is high or a hard override has fired. The trend rule is now the thing to watch.

The crisis atlas is unaffected.' Loaded order (column, gap 14): HeadlinePanel, AnatomyPanel, RedFlagPanel, LadderPanel, HistoryPanel, FusionPanel, EpistemicPanel, then centered footer 'bubblegauge 3.1.0 · Research, not advice.' with DEMO suffix ' · DEMO fixture (offline) — re-verify against the live service'.

Grid auto-fit minmax(300px,1fr). Column 1 title 'Block S · structural fragility', sub 'MAPS TO DEPTH · VALUE 0.711' (eyebrow uppercase; source text 'maps to DEPTH · value 0.711', toFixed(3)), rows s1..s5 in order. Column 2 'Block D · dynamics / trigger', sub source 'maps to TIMING · raw 0.229 → ×V 1 → 0.229' (value_raw toFixed(3), multiplier as-is, value toFixed(3)), rows d1..d4, footer 'V · VIX term-structure multiplier — contango → ×1 (lagging confirmation). When near-term fear exceeds long-term fear, stress is already underway; this confirms, it doesn't predict.' Centered formula 'Score = 100 × S^α × D^β (α=β=0.5) → point 40.35 → Monte Carlo median 40' (α/β superscripts). Rows only render for ids present in indicators (missing id skipped).

slices.notes: Source: /home/user/save-haven-monitor/bubblegauge.jsx (all 1156 lines read) + consumption seams in /home/user/save-haven-monitor/dashboard.jsx (lines 7, 519-537, 574, 838-848, 882, 1064-1067, 1123, 1803-1852). Structural notes for a rewrite: (1) Activation is resolved ONCE at script-parse time (IIFE), before React mounts; enable/disable requires a page load — there is no runtime toggle. sessionStorage persistence is per-tab; an invalid ?status-api value present in the URL disables even when sessionStorage holds a valid key. (2) DEMO mode ('demo' or 'fixture' keys) must make ZERO network calls, hide the per-indicator 'full methodology' links (apiBase is null), and append demo markers (' · demo' on Freshness, ' · demo fixture' on the LIVE BACKFILL header, ' · DEMO fixture (offline) — re-verify against the live service' on the tab footer). (3) State shape of every data hook is {loading, notReady, error, json}; 503 is a distinct 'warming' state, never an error. Module-level cache (25-min TTL) is shared across all hook instances of the same path — Strip and DetailTab share one score fetch only via this cache, not via shared state (they are separate hook instances; without the cache they would double-fetch). Focus revalidation bypasses cache but only commits validated 200s. (4) buildAiLive output shape consumed by dashboard: {a:{lineKey:[[m,-60..0 value]]}, labels:{}, live:{}, asOf:{}, anchorMonth, anchorPartial, computedAt, serviceVersion, metrics, fgSeries}; anchors use month-index-60 as x and drop non-finite/non-positive values; FX inversion (chf, jpy) happens here (1/value); the dashboard rebases per-line with fallback (aiLive.a[key] || static s.a) and swaps legend labels only for live lines. fear_greed is deliberately excluded from that rebasing path. (5) Gotcha: dashboard wires DetailTab goToCrisis={() => setTab('explorer')} — FusionPanel passes the crisis explorer id ('ai2026' etc.) but it is ignored; the button only switches tabs. A faithful rewrite must reproduce this (or a parity test asserting crisis selection would fail against the original). (6) Demo fusion similarities are derived, not stored: fingerprint {valuation:0.92, concentration:0.78, industryRunup:0.61, creditTightness:0.74, leverage:0.30(constant)} -> 2021 87%, 2000 79%, 1929 69%, 2007 58% (RMS gap formula in bg-17). (7) All 'exact_values' for the LIVE BACKFILL pills are format-function outputs of fixture metrics (verified arithmetic), so they double as golden assertions for both the fixture data and the formatters. (8) Rendering quirks worth pinning in tests: block_D sub 'raw 0.229 → ×V 1 → 0.229' renders multiplier 1.0 as '1' (JS number stringification); d1 sub_score 0.543 renders '0.54'; en-dashes (–) in 'IQR 34–47' vs hyphens elsewhere; '·stale' (no leading space) in pills vs ' · stale' in indicator rows. (9) Everything renders with inline styles on the dashboard's dark palette (C tokens) — no CSS classes except tabbar-scroll in dashboard; Recharts components used: ComposedChart/Area/Line/XAxis/YAxis/CartesianGrid/Tooltip/ReferenceLine/ResponsiveContainer (LineChart/ReferenceArea destructured but unused).

critic.verdict_on_coverage: Coverage is strong — the 7-slice digest captures all major surfaces, the frozen data constants, the bubblegauge activation/fetch/validation machinery, and both files' error-isolation boundaries. The confirmed gaps cluster in four areas: (1) STATE-LIFECYCLE contracts — every tab unmounts on switch so all per-tab state resets, tab choice never persists (no hash, no storage beyond the bubblegauge key), and COVID's Bitcoin line ships defaultOff; (2) NEGATIVE/ABSENCE contracts — AiLivePanel, LiveBadge, FearGreedBlock and the judgment span render nothing (not error text) in specific edge states, and HistoryPanel has its own independent loading/empty states; (3) QUIRKS a faithful rewrite could accidentally 'fix' — the crisis-clock chart reuses ChartTip so correlations display as Math.round-ed 0/1 with fmtM month-labels (Unicode minus U+2212, vs ASCII hyphen on axis ticks), and FusionPanel's goToCrisis argument is dropped so the CTA lands on Explorer's default GFC rather than the analogue; (4) SECONDARY AFFORDANCES — native title-attribute tooltips carrying exact strings across ~8 element families, strip keyboard activation (Enter/Space, role=button, only in the loaded state), the demo-only suppression of the 'full methodology ↗' external link, Playbook's M9-default/⛔-chips/unlabeled-<10%-bar-segments, and clickable blank matrix cells with the fallback note. Checked and NOT missed (already covered or genuinely absent): sessionStorage tab persistence (does not exist), URL hash behavior (none), print CSS (none), dashboard.jsx error boundaries (none exist — only index.html's pre-mount fail-visible handler and bubblegauge's Boundary, both in the digest), fmtM/ChartTip/FanTip formats, focus-revalidation, and the ?status-api-off flow.
